operating_state: Third quarter 2023 earnings of $9.1 billion decreased from $19.7 billion in the prior year, driven by lower crude oil and natural gas prices and declining industry refining and chemical margins. Upstream production of 3.7 million oil-equivalent barrels per day remained flat year-over-year, with Permian and Guyana growth offsetting divestments and government mandates.
cost_structure: Capital and exploration expenditures increased to $6.0 billion in the third quarter and $18.6 billion year-to-date, up 5% and 22% respectively from prior year periods, with 2023 capital spending expected at the top end of $23-25 billion guidance range. Operating expenses increased primarily due to higher project and maintenance expenses across segments.
material_events: The Corporation announced definitive agreements to acquire Denbury Inc. for $4.9 billion in July 2023 to accelerate Low Carbon Solutions opportunities and a merger agreement with Pioneer Natural Resources in October 2023 that would more than double Permian production. The company distributed $11.1 billion in dividends and repurchased $13.1 billion of common stock during the first nine months of 2023.
macro_exposures: The Corporation incurred after-tax charges of $0.2 billion related to additional European taxes imposed on the energy sector. Crude oil prices increased in the third quarter due to strong demand, tight inventory levels, and OPEC+ supply limitations, while natural gas prices declined significantly with storage levels above historical averages.
forward_outlook: Management is conducting an annual planning and budgeting process to prioritize assets with highest future value potential while identifying divestment candidates, with Board review expected in the fourth quarter. The Corporation maintains capital allocation priorities of investing in competitively advantaged high-return projects and returning cash to shareholders through consistent share repurchases and growing dividends.
uncertainty_profile: Depending on the outcome of the planning process, certain asset groups could be at risk for impairment based on significant changes to development plans or increased likelihood of divestments. Completion of the Pioneer and Denbury acquisitions remains subject to regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions.
